description: The measurement from side to side; the quality of being wide. 'Lonai' names horizontal extent—how far something spans, how broad something stretches.
sound_symbolism: The word spans level. 'Lo' opens with horizontal extent, span beginning. 'Na' extends across with stable reach. Then 'i' completes the measurement, width known.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo lonai' (the width). Related to adjective 'losha' (wide). The third dimension alongside height and depth.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Width created flow—horizontal designs that guided the eye across surfaces.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Width of view sees more—broad perspective recognizes connection and context.
